institutionName: North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University
collegeName: College of Agriculture and Environmental Sciences (CAES)
programName: Bachelor of Science in Food & Nutritional Sciences — Human Nutrition
programDescription: The Human Nutrition concentration prepares students for careers as registered dietitians, clinical nutritionists, and public health nutrition professionals. Students complete supervised practice hours in clinical, community, and food service settings. The program provides the didactic coursework required for the Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) credential, a pathway to one of the most in-demand healthcare professions.
accreditationBody: ACEND (Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics)

certifications: Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) — requires graduate degree (effective 2024)
careerOutlook.outlook: Demand for dietitians and nutritionists is growing as healthcare increasingly emphasizes prevention and chronic disease management through nutrition. Note: Starting 2024, a master's degree is required for RDN eligibility — the BS prepares students for this graduate pathway.
